About Me

Analytical mind,
product instinct

I'm an MBA Finance student at Global Institute of Management Sciences, Bengaluru, with a BBA background (CGPA 8.76) from Mahila Samaja Degree College, Kolar. My work sits at the intersection of financial rigor and product strategy.

During my internship at SJS Enterprises, I applied Excel and Power BI to improve reporting efficiency by 20% and improve campaign targeting effectiveness by ~15%. Currently, my capstone project explores AI for financial inclusion — helping daily wage and gig workers access digital financial services.

I am pursuing a certification in Product Management with Generative & Agentic AI at BITS School of Management — reflecting my belief that the future of finance is deeply tied to intelligent product design.

Projects

Work that demonstrates impact

Finance · Research · MBA Internship Project Report
Commodity Derivatives & Agricultural Impact Study
Jan 2026 – March 2026  ·  Regression Analysis  ·  8 Commodities Studied
A full-scale research report examining how macroeconomic variables — exchange rate, GDP, inflation, interest rate, foreign reserves, CPI, FDI, and unemployment — drive price volatility across 8 major Indian commodities: Gold, Silver, Crude Oil, Natural Gas, Copper, Aluminium, Mentha Oil, and Cardamom. Conducted at the Institute of Financial Markets (SEBI-registered, associated with Angel One Limited).
★ Precious Metals
Gold & Silver showed limited correlation with domestic macroeconomic variables — confirmed as global safe-haven assets independent of national fundamentals.
★ Energy Commodities
Crude Oil volatility was strongly linked to foreign reserves. Natural Gas was sensitive to exchange rate & CPI — reflecting India's import dependence and domestic inflationary pressures.
★ Industrial Metals
Copper (R²: 0.72) and Aluminium (R²: 0.65) were driven by global trade & industrial demand rather than domestic indicators — acting as barometers of global growth.
★ Agricultural Commodities
Mentha Oil (R²: 0.75) — FDI & foreign reserves were key drivers. Cardamom (R²: 0.68) — sensitive to FDI & GDP, reflecting export-oriented agricultural trade.
Notable Correlation Findings
Gold ↔ FDI: r = 0.96 Crude Oil ↔ Unemployment: r = 0.73 Copper ↔ Inflation: r = 0.76 Silver ↔ Inflation: r = 0.64 Natural Gas ↔ Ex. Rate: r = 0.53 Aluminium ↔ CPI: r = 0.62
  • Regression analysis across 8 commodities using 8 macroeconomic variables (20 annual observations each)
  • Overall conclusion: global forces (exchange rate, foreign reserves) outweigh domestic variables in driving Indian commodity volatility
  • Proposed policy recommendations: stabilize exchange rates, maintain foreign reserves, strengthen commodity derivatives oversight
  • Research conducted under Institute of Financial Markets — SEBI-registered, Angel One partnership
Regression Analysis Excel Macroeconomic Research Commodity Markets Risk Management MCX / NCDEX
Fintech · AI · Capstone · BITS School of Management — PM with Gen & Agentic AI
XENORA — AI for Financial Inclusion
February 2026 – Present  ·  Team of 6  ·  BITS School of Management Capstone
A comprehensive product strategy and research project addressing how AI can improve financial inclusion for India's 490 million informal workers — daily wage labourers, gig workers, and domestic helpers — who are excluded from formal financial systems by language, literacy, documentation, and dignity barriers. Applied ERRC, SCAMPER, Brainstorming, and Business Model frameworks across 5 research documents.
Core Problem Statement
"Daily wage labourers face complete digital exclusion from financial guidance when apps, forms, and advisory content are designed exclusively in English with text-heavy interfaces — the most financially vulnerable population cannot access the tools built in their name."
490M informal workers · TAM: $3.5 trillion (GSMA) · Only 27% of Indian adults have basic financial literacy
ERRC Framework
Eliminate: English-only interfaces, formal documentation prerequisites, jargon
Create: Voice-first vernacular AI, real-time predatory loan detector, parametric micro-insurance, government benefits navigator
SCAMPER Framework
Substitute: CIBIL scores → psychometric + behavioural credit scoring
Adapt: WhatsApp voice-note UX → financial guidance interface
Reverse: AI proactively finds Raju — not Raju finds AI
Business Model
B2C Freemium: ₹29–49/user/month
B2B SaaS: MFI/NGO white-label — ₹5–15/beneficiary
B2G: ₹50–100 per welfare scheme activation
SOM Year 1: 200K users · Year 3: 5M users
Problem Signal Discovery
Analysed real user complaints from Instagram, Swiggy & WhatsApp through the daily wage labourer lens — 10 reviews, 4 repeating patterns: broken support systems, no accountability, open scammer access, and unfair account blocking
Evidence Base
41% higher task completion — vernacular AI (ArXiv 2025) ₹22,845 Cr lost to financial fraud in India (2024) 490M informal workers — NITI Aayog 2025 86% longer sessions with voice AI (ArXiv 2025) Literacy — Asia's #1 digital barrier (GSMA 2024) Real competitor: moneylenders at 60%+ APR
  • Designed 5 core product features: vernacular voice AI, predatory loan detector, government benefits navigator, UPI micro-savings automation, and alternative credit scoring
  • Built 6-phase structured brainstorming with prioritised action plan; top prototype: WhatsApp chatbot in Kannada for loan term explanation
  • Defined North Star Metric: Monthly Active Voice Users (MAVU) — voice interaction is inclusion; passive account ownership is not
  • Non-negotiable design principles: explainability, offline-first, data minimalism, dignity as a product requirement
AI / Fintech Product Strategy ERRC Framework SCAMPER Business Model Canvas Financial Inclusion UX Research Voice AI
